title: cyanea superba subsp. regina
intro: Cyanea superba subsp. regina, also known as the Hawaiian lobeliad, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the island of Hawaii. It is a unique and valuable plant that has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Cyanea superba subsp. regina typically involve drying and processing the plant's leaves and flowers to create teas, infusions, and topical applications.
